semanticCore: Aorta, life-artery, main cardiovascular connection, or never-ending stream of water

keyDerivations.meaning: Aorta, the main artery connected directly to the heart

figurativeOrRareUsages: ثُمَّ لَقَطَعْنَا مِنْهُ الْوَتِينَ (69:46): Then We would have cut from him the aorta/life-artery (severing life instantly).
discardedNoise: Watan (homeland) - different spelling W-T-N vs. W-T'-N.
summaryEn: The root refers to the aorta or life-artery (Wateen), used to describe the absolute physical destruction God would inflict upon the Prophet if he were to fabricate revelations.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الوتين وهو عرق لاصق بالظهر يجري منه الدم للقلب فإذا انقطع مات صاحبه (وتن)، مصوراً العقوبة الإلهية بقطع وتين الكاذب المفترض على الله.
rawText: الوَتِينُ [The aorta: or the aorta descendens:] a certain vein [or artery] adhering to the inner side of the backbone all along, which supplies all the [other] veins [or arteries] with blood, and irrigates the flesh, being the river of the body: or a certain thick white vein resembling a cane: [this last is the description given by Zj in his “ Khalk el-Insán: ”] or [the aorta ascendens;] the نِيَاط of the heart: or a certain white vein within the back of the neck: it is said to draw up [its supply] from the heart, and in it is the blood. === Also, the خِلْب, q. v.: pl. أَوْتِنَةٌ and وُتُنٌ: (M:) i. q. نِيَاطُ القَلْبِ. (Bd, and Jel, lxix. 45.) See أَبْهَرُ.
